title: Claim an unbooked seat with INSERT ... RETURNING
prompt_md:
Booking is really just claiming a seat. Seat `3` (label `A3`, showtime `1`) is currently unbooked. Insert a new row into `bookings` claiming it for user `2` (Ben):

- `id` = `5`, `user_id` = `2`, `showtime_id` = `1`, `seat_id` = `3`, `created_at` = `'2026-06-03 10:00:00'`

Return the new booking's `id` and `seat_id`. The `RETURNING` clause confirms exactly what got claimed — in a real system you'd add a uniqueness rule on `seat_id` so a second insert for the same seat fails.
hints: List the columns in order: (id, user_id, showtime_id, seat_id, created_at).
Values are (5, 2, 1, 3, '2026-06-03 10:00:00'); return id and seat_id.
reference_solution: INSERT INTO bookings (id, user_id, showtime_id, seat_id, created_at) VALUES (5, 2, 1, 3, '2026-06-03 10:00:00') RETURNING id, seat_id;
